page: Amazon Web Services c8g.48xlarge is an ARM64-based compute-optimized server powered by 192 dedicated physical cores of AWS Neoverse-V2 processors running at 2.7 GHz. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance features 384 GB of memory, translating to a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for external data transfers.

In benchmark evaluations, the c8g.48xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance in multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations. The server achieves top 10% rankings in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Redis SET operations, which reached 355.18 million operations per second. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with uncached read and write speeds scoring in the top tier. However, single-core performance, such as Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core tests, remains in the average tier.

The ARM64 architecture provides high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized workloads. The lack of hyperthreading, with one thread per physical core, ensures predictable performance for compute-intensive tasks. This server is highly suited for large-scale web serving, software compilation, database operations, and multi-threaded data processing, though it is less optimal for workloads requiring high single-core speeds or local NVMe storage.
description: Amazon Web Services c8g.48xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server featuring 192 dedicated physical cores on AWS Neoverse-V2 processors running at 2.7 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 384 GB of memory, a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and no local storage or GPUs. The server excels in multi-threaded and memory-intensive tasks, achieving top-tier results in stress-ng multi-core performance, uncached memory bandwidth, and Redis SET operations. Single-core performance is moderate, placing in the average tier. This instance offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for parallelized workloads, making it highly suitable for high-throughput database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.48xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server with 192 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 384 GB RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.

tagline: A high-density, multi-core ARM64 server delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-threaded performance for compute-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated ARM64 physical cores using AWS Neoverse-V2 processors at 2.7 GHz.
384 GB of system memory providing a balanced 2.0 GB per core ratio.
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and Redis database performance.
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput external connectivity.
Optimized for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, and static web serving.
